introSection: Afterburn, originally known as Top Gun: The Jet Coaster, is a inverted roller coaster located at Carowinds in Charlotte, North Carolina, USA. It opened on March 20, 1999 and is an Inverted Coaster installation from Bolliger & Mabillard.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
In early 1997, Carowinds began consultations with Swiss roller coaster manufacturer Bolliger & Mabillard about adding a new ride to the park. During 18 months of discussions, several designs and themes for the ride were developed, including theming it to the Godzilla franchise. In July 1998, the park announced the addition of Top Gun: The Jet Coaster for the 1999 season. At a cost of $10.5 million, it would be the single biggest investment in the park's history. The ride opened on March 20, 1999.

Top Gun: The Jet Coaster became Afterburn in 2008 after the park was sold to Cedar Fair in 2006. For the 2014 season, Afterburn was repainted.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Afterburn stands tall, has a drop of  and reaches speeds of. It features a track layout and a nearly three-minute-long ride time. It also has six inversions, including a batwing. The other inverted coaster with this element is Montu at Busch Gardens Tampa. Afterburn has generally been well received, having been featured several times as a top 50 roller coaster in the Amusement Today Golden Ticket Awards.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: Before dispatching, "Clear for takeoff" will be occasionally heard. After rolling out of the station, the train climbs the tall chain lift hill. At the top, riders drop to the right, reaching a max speed of. Then, the train moves through a tall vertical loop. Riders head into a small ravine, which leads into an immelmann. The train flips into a zero-g roll underneath the chain lift hill. After this, riders approach a batwing with a tunnel in between. Following the batwing, the train goes through an airtime hill. Riders whip through a corkscrew, before heading into a helix. The helix is followed by the final brake run. After a right turn, the train heads back into the station.

fullText:
Afterburn, originally known as Top Gun: The Jet Coaster, is a inverted roller coaster located at Carowinds in Charlotte, North Carolina, USA. It opened on March 20, 1999 and is an Inverted Coaster installation from Bolliger & Mabillard.

In early 1997, Carowinds began consultations with Swiss roller coaster manufacturer Bolliger & Mabillard about adding a new ride to the park. During 18 months of discussions, several designs and themes for the ride were developed, including theming it to the Godzilla franchise. In July 1998, the park announced the addition of Top Gun: The Jet Coaster for the 1999 season. At a cost of $10.5 million, it would be the single biggest investment in the park's history. The ride opened on March 20, 1999.

Top Gun: The Jet Coaster became Afterburn in 2008 after the park was sold to Cedar Fair in 2006. For the 2014 season, Afterburn was repainted.

Afterburn stands tall, has a drop of  and reaches speeds of. It features a track layout and a nearly three-minute-long ride time. It also has six inversions, including a batwing. The other inverted coaster with this element is Montu at Busch Gardens Tampa. Afterburn has generally been well received, having been featured several times as a top 50 roller coaster in the Amusement Today Golden Ticket Awards.

Before dispatching, "Clear for takeoff" will be occasionally heard. After rolling out of the station, the train climbs the tall chain lift hill. At the top, riders drop to the right, reaching a max speed of. Then, the train moves through a tall vertical loop. Riders head into a small ravine, which leads into an immelmann. The train flips into a zero-g roll underneath the chain lift hill. After this, riders approach a batwing with a tunnel in between. Following the batwing, the train goes through an airtime hill. Riders whip through a corkscrew, before heading into a helix. The helix is followed by the final brake run. After a right turn, the train heads back into the station.
